name: Apotropaic ward ob3ect: a protective magical object (horseshoe, nazar, hamsa, witch bottle, or threshold mark) that deflects malevolent forces. FSPLIT: the ward intercepts the incoming curse or evil eye, splitting the malicious intention from its target — the ward catches what was meant for the protected person. FFUSE: the ward neutralizes and grounds the intercepted malice, reconstituting the situation as safety — the threat is dissolved, not merely deflected. VINIT: the unprotected threshold. TANCH: the ward itself as boundary object. AFWD: the curse approaches. AREV: the curse is turned back. ENGAGR: the ward is simultaneously a physical object (glass, iron, herbs) and a spiritual agent (it acts with apparent intention).

phase_0.domain_name: Apotropaic ward ob3ect: a protective magical object (horseshoe, nazar, hamsa, witch bottle, or threshold mark) that deflects malevolent forces. FSPLIT: the ward intercepts the incoming curse or evil eye, splitting the malicious intention from its target — the ward catches what was meant for the protected person. FFUSE: the ward neutralizes and grounds the intercepted malice, reconstituting the situation as safety — the threat is dissolved, not merely deflected. VINIT: the unprotected threshold. TANCH: the ward itself as boundary object. AFWD: the curse approaches. AREV: the curse is turned back. ENGAGR: the ward is simultaneously a physical object (glass, iron, herbs) and a spiritual agent (it acts with apparent intention).
phase_0.boundary_condition: the ward itself as boundary object

VINIT.justification: void/uninitialized state before any ward is placed

TANCH.chosen_element: the ward itself as boundary object
TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that contains and defines the protective system

AFWD.justification: forward morphism of malevolent force toward target

AREV.justification: reverse/descent transformation of threat away from target

CLINK.justification: composition of interception and neutralization into a single protective act

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the ward's identity as protective object
IMSCRIB.justification: self-recognition: the ward knows itself as a defender

FSPLIT.justification: splits malicious intention from target, catching what was meant for protected person

FFUSE.chosen_element: ward neutralizes and grounds intercepted malice
FFUSE.justification: reconstitutes situation as safety, threat dissolved

EVALF.justification: negative branch: ward ineffective or overwhelmed

ENGAGR.chosen_element: ward is simultaneously physical object and spiritual agent
ENGAGR.justification: paradox: material and intentional agency held together

IFIX.chosen_element: the ward's protective enchantment or blessing
IFIX.justification: permanent irreversible record of protective intent

phase_2.fuse_element: ward neutralizes and grounds intercepted malice

phase_3.false_description: ward broken or absent, threat incoming
phase_3.both_description: ward is both physical object and spiritual agent simultaneously

steps.domain_action: Step 1 IMSCRIB: the ward recognizes itself as a protective object
Step 2 AREV:   the ward descends into the material world (e.g., forged, hung)
Step 3 FSPLIT: the ward intercepts an incoming curse, splitting threat from target
Step 4 AFWD:   the curse's energy is redirected away from the protected
Step 5 FFUSE:  the ward neutralizes the intercepted malice, reconstituting safety
Step 6 CLINK:  the acts of interception and neutralization compose into one protective cycle
Step 7 IFIX:   the protective enchantment is permanently fixed in the ward
Step 8 IMSCRIB: the ward closes the cycle, reaffirming its identity as guardian

phase_5.compiler_frontend: intention of the maker or user
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: sympathetic resonance between ward and threat
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the ward's physical form and enchantment
phase_5.alfs_store: the original apotropaic tradition or formula

phase_6.cycle_cost: the ward's material integrity and enchantment charge
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the ward's protective action conserves the balance between threat and safety
